explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey, man, I've been thinking about this a lot lately. You know how everyone says money can't buy happiness? I totally disagree. Like, straight up, money is the key to everything. If you're rich, you can buy experiences, security, even friends if you want. Poor people are miserable because they worry all the time. What do you think? Whoa, that's a strong take, Alex. I mean, yeah, money helps with basics like food and a roof, but happiness? Isn't it more about relationships or purpose? I've known rich people who are super depressed. Nah, see, those rich depressed people? They're just bad at spending it. Like, they buy dumb stuff or hang with fake people. If you know how to use money right—travel, therapy, whatever—you're set. Poor folks can't even afford therapy. It's all about options, dude. Hmm, options... okay, fair. But, uh, what about lottery winners? Some go broke and miserable. Or billionaires who off themselves. Like, that one tech guy... uh, not naming names. Lottery winners are idiots, mostly. They don't know how to manage it. And those suicides? Probably mental health issues that money can't fix alone, but c'mon, it gives you the best shot. Imagine being poor AND depressed—no doctors, no escapes. Money buys freedom from that grind.
